steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: "Main purpose" questions require identifying what the entire passage is doing as a whole — not a single detail, but the overarching reason the text was written.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — To summarize Emerson's biography.: the passage mentions only that Emerson lived at the Old Manse and wrote "Nature" there. It does not trace his life story. ✗
  • Choice B — To explain why readers enjoy Emerson's works.: the passage says visitors want to feel "connected" to an author they admire, but never explains what makes his writings enjoyable or appealing. ✗
  • Choice C — To describe a museum focused on Emerson's life and works.: the passage introduces the Old Manse as a museum, explains who visits and why, and details how curators promote connection to Emerson through trails and activities. Every sentence serves this purpose. ✓
  • Choice D — To compare the Old Manse with other museums.: no other museum is mentioned anywhere in the passage. ✗
That matches Choice C — To describe a museum focused on Emerson's life and works..
